---
package: "openpipeline_spatial"
version: "v0.5.0"
title: "from_h5mu_to_spatialdata"
namespace: "Convert"
description: "Reads in an H5MU file and saves it as a SpatialData Zarr store."
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline_spatial v0.5.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `from_h5mu_to_spatialdata`  
Namespace: `convert`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial/tree/v0.5.0/src/convert/from_h5mu_to_spatialdata)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/components/convert/from_h5mu_to_spatialdata)

:::

The selected
modality in the MuData is stored as the main table in the SpatialData object. If a matching existing SpatialData is provided, it will be used to fill the
remaining SpatialData slots.   



## Argument group

### Arguments

|Name                  |Description                                                                        |Attributes                                 |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--input`             |Input H5MU file.                                                                   |`file`, required, example: `"input.h5mu"`  |
|`--input_spatialdata` |An optional existing SpatialData Zarr store to fill remaining slots from.          |`file`, example: `"existing.zarr"`         |
|`--modality`          |The modality in the MuData to be used as the main table in the SpatialData object. |`string`, default: `"rna"`                 |
|`--output`            |The path to the output SpatialData Zarr store.                                     |`file`, required, example: `"output.zarr"` |

## Authors


  * Dorien Roosen [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/dorien-er) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/dorien-roosen) (maintainer)

  * Luke Zappia [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/lazappi) [{{< fa brands orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0001-7744-8565) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/lazappi) (author)

## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial \
  -r v0.5.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/convert/from_h5mu_to_spatialdata/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/components/convert/from_h5mu_to_spatialdata).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial \
  -r v0.5.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/convert/from_h5mu_to_spatialdata/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).
